expectations.text: Body contains at least 6 Markdown internal links [text](/path)
expectations.evidence: 9 Markdown links present (6 ingredient links + 3 recipe links), though 6 use awkward 'Read more on the [Ginger ingredient page]' format
No tradition links. References TCM and Ayurveda by name but never links to /traditions/ayurveda or /traditions/traditional-chinese-medicine

expectations.text: Post contains at least 2 recipe links (/recipes/...)
expectations.evidence: 3 recipe links: roganjosh, whole-garam-masala, kheer — agent claimed these were verified from recipe files

expectations.text: YAML frontmatter has title, slug, tags, and meta_description
expectations.evidence: All 4 basic fields present, but tags are overly verbose (12 tags with multi-word entries like 'warming spices', 'winter cooking')

expectations.text: Body does not open with a # H1 heading (page template renders the title)
expectations.evidence: Line 21: '# The Warming Spice Cabinet: What TCM and Ayurveda Know About Cold-Weather Cooking' — duplicate H1

expectations.text: Post includes a FAQ section with at least 2 questions
expectations.evidence: No FAQ section. Ends with 'A Note on Proportions and Caution' and a boilerplate disclaimer.

expectations.text: Post does not end with a boilerplate disclaimer paragraph
expectations.evidence: Line 169: 'Ancient Pantry presents traditional food knowledge from TCM, Ayurveda...Traditional claims and research findings are presented separately. Nothing here constitutes medical advice.'

expectations.text: YAML includes an internal_links_used audit field
Internal links use natural anchor text (e.g. [ginger] not [Ginger ingredient page])
expectations.evidence: Lines 58, 72, 88, 102, 116, 128 all use 'Read more on the [Ginger ingredient page]' — awkward, unnatural anchor text that reads poorly in context
